title: Implement TUI Help Overlay Toggle
description: Update the Textual TUI runtime and supporting helpers in `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` to add a compact help overlay/modal, bind `?` to toggle it, bind `Esc` to close it, allow `?` to close it when already open, and keep normal refresh, navigation, and merge behavior unchanged when the overlay is not active.
acceptance_criteria: `?` opens a visible shortcuts help overlay from the TUI.
The overlay includes entries for `j/k` and arrows, `f`, `r`, `m`, `Enter`, and `q`.
`Esc` closes the overlay and `?` also closes it when already open.
Closing the overlay returns the operator to the prior interaction state without resetting unrelated runtime state.
Existing TUI behavior outside the overlay remains unchanged, including refresh and merge handling.
Footer includes a concise help hint such as `? help`.

handoff_summary.completed: Added footer help text, introduced runtime overlay visibility helpers, and wired a Textual modal overlay that shows the requested shortcuts and closes on `?` or `Esc`. The overlay blocks unrelated in-app key handling while active and leaves selection, refresh state, and merge state intact when dismissed.
handoff_summary.remaining: A follow-up outside this bead's file scope is still needed to update the existing orchestrator test expectation for the footer string so it includes the new `? help` suffix.
handoff_summary.risks: `python -m unittest tests.test_tui` passes, but `python -m unittest tests.test_tui tests.test_orchestrator` still has one failure in `tests/test_orchestrator.py` because that test expects the old footer text. No runtime failure was found in `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` itself.
handoff_summary.next_action: Update the stale orchestrator footer assertion to match the new `? help` footer text, then rerun the combined TUI/orchestrator test target.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Downstream agents editing `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` should preserve the new `question_mark` binding, modal close behavior, and footer hint together so the help flow stays internally consistent.

conflict_risks: Downstream agents editing `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` should preserve the new `question_mark` binding, modal close behavior, and footer hint together so the help flow stays internally consistent.

execution_history.summary: Implemented the TUI help overlay toggle in `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py`, including `?` open/close, `Esc` close, modal shortcut content, and a `? help` footer hint without changing normal refresh or merge flow when the overlay is inactive.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run targeted validation needed to confirm the implementation.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
